title: 10 best herbal teas for stye
intro_desc: Herbal teas for stye are infusions made from plants and herbs that can help alleviate the symptoms of a stye, which is an inflammation of the oil glands in the eyelid. These teas can reduce inflammation, fight bacteria, and promote healing. Some examples of herbal teas that help with stye include chamomile, calendula, and goldenseal. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and redness, while calendula has antiseptic and wound-healing properties that can help speed up the healing process. Goldenseal is a powerful antibacterial herb that can help prevent infection. By using these herbal teas, people with styes can experience relief from painful symptoms, faster healing times, and a reduced risk of complications. This can improve their quality of life by allowing them to resume their normal activities and reducing the need for prescription medications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with stye because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with styes. The tea is made from the Euphrasia officinalis plant, which has been used traditionally for treating various eye conditions. The astringent properties of eyebright tea can help to dry out the stye and promote healing. Additionally, the tea can help to soothe irritation and itching, providing relief to the affected area. Drinking eyebright tea may also boost the immune system, helping the body to fight off the infection that causes styes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like tannins and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, providing relief from stye symptoms.
Antimicrobial: Eyebright tea has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of phenolic acids and essential oils, helping to combat the bacterial infection that causes styes.
Astringent: The astringent properties of eyebright tea, attributed to its tannin content, help to dry out the stye and promote healing by reducing excess oil and fluid in the affected area.
Analgesic: Eyebright tea contains analgesic compounds such as salicylic acid, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with a stye.
Immune-boosting: Eyebright tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C and zinc, that support the immune system and aid in fighting off the infection that causes styes.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried eyebright herb.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, then let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Squeeze a slice of lemon into the tea for added vitamin C and antioxidants.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, until the stye heals.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in therapeutic compounds, eyebright flowers are commonly used in teas for stye due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Leaves: High in tannins, eyebright leaves contribute to the tea's potency by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in stye-affected areas.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils, eyebright seeds can help alleviate symptoms of stye when used in medicinal teas, thanks to their soothing and antibacterial effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy tea helps with stye because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria causing the stye. The tea is made from dried daisy flowers and has been used traditionally to treat various skin conditions, including styes. The flavonoids and other compounds found in daisy tea can help to soothe irritation, reduce redness, and promote healing of the affected area. Drinking daisy tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with styes. Additionally, daisy tea may help to prevent the recurrence of styes by boosting the immune system and improving overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Daisy tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids, that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyelid, providing relief from stye symptoms.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of daisy tea, due to its content of phenolic acids, can help combat the bacterial infection causing the stye, promoting healing.
Astringent: Daisy tea has astringent properties, which can help dry out the pus-filled bump of a stye, aiding in its faster disappearance.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in daisy tea, from flavonoids and other polyphenols, can help protect the eye area from further damage and support overall eye health.
Lymphatic system support: Daisy tea may help stimulate the lymphatic system, which can assist in draining the excess fluid accumulated in the stye, reducing its size and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried daisy flowers and 1 cup of water.
Rinse the daisy flowers to remove any impurities.
Boil the water and add the dried daisy flowers.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down before using it as a warm compress for the stye.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and steroids, daisy flowers are used in medicinal teas for stye due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Containing tannins and essential oils, daisy leaves are used in teas to soothe irritation and reduce swelling associated with stye.
Buds: High in antioxidants, daisy buds are used in medicinal teas for stye to help accelerate healing and alleviate symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria that cause styes. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which contain flavonoids and carotenoids that have healing and soothing effects on the skin. Drinking pot marigold tea or using it as a compress on the affected area can help relieve the pain, swelling, and redness associated with styes. Additionally, the tea can help speed up the healing process and prevent the spread of infection to other parts of the eye.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, providing relief from stye discomfort. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids, such as lutein and zeaxanthin.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold tea help fight bacterial infections that can cause or worsen styes, thanks to its active constituents like volatile oils and alkaloids.

Wound-healing: The wound-healing properties of pot marigold tea promote tissue regeneration and help the stye heal faster, as it contains tannins, saponins, and polysaccharides that contribute to skin repair.

Analgesic: Pot marigold tea's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with styes by inhibiting the sensation of pain, which can be attributed to its flavonoid and triterpene content.

Immune-boosting: The immune-boosting properties of pot marigold tea support the body's ability to fight off infections and promote overall eye health, due to the presence of antioxidants, such as vitamin C and carotenoids.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water and add two teaspoons of dried pot marigold flowers.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, until the stye heals.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are used in medicinal teas for stye due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are often used in teas to help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds can be used in teas to help soothe irritation and promote healing of styes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Chamomile contains flavonoids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce the swelling and redness associated with a stye. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the bacteria that cause styes. Drinking chamomile tea or using it as a compress can help soothe the affected area and promote healing. However, it is important to note that while chamomile can help alleviate the symptoms of a stye, it is not a substitute for medical treatment, especially if the stye is severe or does not improve over time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains the anti-inflammatory compound chamazulene, which helps reduce swelling and redness in the eyelid caused by a stye.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of German chamomile tea, due to its content of active constituents like α-bisabolol and matricin, can help fight the bacterial infection causing the stye.
Wound healing: German chamomile tea promotes wound healing, thanks to its flavonoids and essential oils, which can speed up the recovery process of a stye.
Pain relief: The analgesic properties of German chamomile tea, attributed to its volatile oils, can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with a stye.

Soothing effects: The soothing effects of German chamomile tea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, can provide relief from itching, irritation, and inflammation caused by a stye.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried German chamomile flowers, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, cover and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, until the stye heals.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them ideal for treating stye.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain therapeutic properties that can help soothe and reduce the irritation caused by stye.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a great source of antioxidants and essential oils, which can help alleviate the symptoms of stye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with stye because it contains antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and fight off the bacteria causing the stye. The herbal tea is made from the leaves of the plantain plant, which are rich in nutrients and compounds that can promote healing and soothe irritation. Drinking plantain tea or using it as a compress can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with a stye, while also reducing redness and inflammation. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties can help draw out any infection and promote drainage, allowing the stye to heal faster.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea contains compounds such as flavonoids and tannins that help reduce inflammation, which is important for stye as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and pain.
Antimicrobial: Plantain tea has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of compounds like phenolic acids and saponins, which help combat the bacterial infection causing stye.
Antioxidant: Plantain tea is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene that help protect the eye area from oxidative stress and support the healing process for stye.
Astringent: The astringent properties of plantain tea, attributed to tannins, help reduce excess secretions and soothe irritation, providing relief from stye symptoms.
Demulcent: Plantain tea has demulcent properties due to its mucilage content, forming a protective layer on the skin and reducing discomfort caused by stye.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2-3 large plantain leaves ( Plantago major or Plantago lanceolata) from a clean, pesticide-free source.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then tear them into small pieces and place them in a pot.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot, bring it to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the plant material, then let it cool down to a warm temperature.
Drink 1/2 cup of the herbal plantain tea, 3-4 times a day, until the stye resolves (usually within a few days).
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, plantain leaves are commonly used to make medicinal teas for treating stye.
Seeds: Plantain seeds are a great source of mucilage, which helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritation caused by stye.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, plantain flowers are used in teas to help alleviate symptoms of stye.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce swelling and redness in stye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrowleaf plantain tea helps with stye because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain. The tea is made from the leaves of the plantain plant, which contain compounds called flavonoids and tannins that have been shown to fight infection and promote healing. Drinking the tea or using it as a compress can help to soothe the affected area and speed up the recovery process. Additionally, plantain tea is a natural remedy that is free from the harsh chemicals found in many over-the-counter treatments, making it a safe and effective option for people of all ages.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Narrowleaf plantain tea helps reduce inflammation in the eye, providing relief from stye-related swelling and redness, due to its active constituents like flavonoids and tannins.
Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits bacterial growth, including Staphylococcus aureus, which can cause styes, thanks to its antibacterial properties from compounds like aucubin and caffeic acid.
Analgesic: Narrowleaf plantain tea can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with styes by numbing the affected area, owing to its analgesic properties from compounds like allantoin.
Astringent: The tea's astringent properties, derived from tannins, help constrict blood vessels and tighten skin around the stye, reducing its size and promoting healing.

Immune-boosting: Narrowleaf plantain tea supports the immune system, aiding the body in fighting off infections and promoting overall eye health, due to its immune-boosting constituents like vitamin C and polysaccharides.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of narrowleaf plantain leaves, rinse, and chop.
Place chopped leaves in a tea infuser and steep in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Remove tea infuser and let the liquid cool down to a warm temperature.
Add raw honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea, squeezing out any remaining liquid from the plantain leaves. Repeat up to three times a day for stye relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the narrowleaf plantain plant are often used to make medicinal teas for stye due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Seeds: Narrowleaf plantain seeds are rich in mucilage, making them useful for soothing inflamed skin and reducing stye symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the narrowleaf plantain plant contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them a good choice for medicinal teas for stye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with stye because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling and fight infection. The tea is made from the leaves of the comfrey plant, which is rich in allantoin, a substance that promotes cell growth and helps to heal wounds. When used as a warm compress, comfrey tea can soothe the pain and irritation caused by a stye, while also reducing redness and inflammation. Additionally, the tea can help to draw out any pus or debris accumulated in the eyelid gland, promoting faster healing. Drinking comfrey tea may also provide internal benefits, as it can help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation throughout the body.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea contains allantoin, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from stye.

Wound healing: Comfrey tea has been found to promote wound healing, which can help the stye heal faster and reduce the risk of infection. This is attributed to allantoin and rosmarinic acid present in comfrey.

Pain relief: Comfrey tea has analgesic properties, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with styes. The main constituents responsible for this are mucilage, tannins, and phenolic acids.

Antibacterial: Comfrey tea has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the spread of infection and reduce the risk of complications from styes. The antibacterial properties come from rosmarinic acid and tannins found in comfrey.
Reduces redness: Comfrey tea's anti-inflammatory and soothing properties help reduce redness and irritation around the stye, promoting faster healing and providing relief.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried comfrey leaves with 1 cup of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the comfrey leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature before using it as a compress on the stye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Comfrey leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for stye due to their high content of allantoin, a substance known to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Comfrey roots contain high levels of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating styes.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas for stye.
Rhyzomes: Comfrey rhyzomes contain mucilage, which can help soothe and protect irritated skin around the stye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed tea helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with styes. Chickweed is also rich in vitamins and minerals that can boost the immune system and promote healing. Drinking chickweed tea can help reduce inflammation from the inside out, while applying a cool compress made from chickweed tea can provide additional relief and speed up the healing process. Additionally, chickweed tea may help prevent the recurrence of styes by keeping the eyelids clean and free of bacteria.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chickweed tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, providing relief from the discomfort caused by a stye. This is due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of chickweed tea help fight off bacterial infections, including those caused by Staphylococcus aureus, which is often responsible for styes. This is attributed to the saponins, flavonoids, and tannins found in chickweed.

Analgesic: Chickweed tea has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with a stye. This is due to the presence of various compounds, including triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids.

Demulcent: Chickweed tea has a soothing effect on irritated tissues, creating a protective barrier and reducing inflammation. This is because of the mucilage content in chickweed, which forms a gel-like layer on the skin.

Astringent: The astringent properties of chickweed tea help to dry out any discharge and promote healing. This is due to the tannins present in chickweed, which cause proteins to coagulate and tighten skin tissues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of fresh chickweed herb or 1 tablespoon of dried chickweed.
Rinse the chickweed thoroughly under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the chickweed in a teapot and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over it.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink it while it is still warm, up to three times a day until the stye heals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, chickweed leaves are commonly used to make medicinal teas for stye due to their soothing effects on inflamed skin.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and other beneficial compounds, chickweed flowers are used in medicinal teas for stye to help reduce redness and irritation.
Stems: High in saponins and other active ingredients, chickweed stems are included in medicinal teas for stye to promote healing and alleviate discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Heartsease tea helps with stye because it contains antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria causing the stye. The herb, also known as wild pansy, has been used traditionally to treat various skin conditions, including styes. The tea can be applied topically to the affected area to soothe irritation and promote healing. Additionally, heartsease tea is rich in antioxidants that can help boost the immune system and speed up the recovery process. Drinking the tea may also provide internal benefits, such as reducing inflammation throughout the body. Overall, heartsease tea is a natural and effective remedy for treating styes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Heartsease tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and tannins, which help reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, providing relief from stye symptoms.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of heartsease tea, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids, help combat bacterial infections causing styes, promoting healing.

Analgesic: Heartsease tea has natural pain-relieving properties, thanks to the presence of compounds like luteolin and apigenin, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with styes.
Astringent: The astringent properties of heartsease tea, attributed to tannins and flavonoids, help shrink and tighten the skin around the stye, reducing its size and promoting faster healing.

Antioxidant: Heartsease tea contains potent antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which support the immune system and help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of stye recurrence.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried heartsease herb.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, then let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the heartsease tea daily, starting from the onset of stye.
Discard any leftover tea after 24 hours.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Heartsease flowers are used in medicinal teas for stye due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Heartsease leaves are often included in teas for stye, as they have been found to have antibacterial and antimicrobial effects.
Seeds: The seeds of the heartsease plant are used in teas to help reduce swelling and promote healing in styes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog rose tea helps with stye because it contains high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and strengthen the immune system. The antibacterial and antimicrobial properties of dog rose tea can also help to fight off the infection causing the stye. Additionally, the astringent properties of dog rose tea can help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes. Drinking dog rose tea or using it as a compress can provide relief and promote healing of the stye.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dog rose tea contains tannins and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from stye discomfort.

Antibacterial: The high vitamin C content and other bioactive compounds in dog rose tea, such as phenolic acids, have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing of a stye.

Antioxidant: Dog rose tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, supporting the overall health of the eyes and reducing the risk of stye complications.
Astringent: The tannins present in dog rose tea act as an astringent, helping to tighten and soothe the skin around the stye, reducing redness and irritation.

Immune-boosting: Dog rose tea is known for its immune-boosting properties due to its high vitamin C content and other antioxidants, which can help the body fight off infections and promote faster recovery from a stye.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of fresh dog rose petals with 2 cups of water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea through a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove petals, and let it cool down to a warm temperature.
Administer 1 teaspoon of the prepared dog rose tea every few hours, directly applying it to the stye using a clean dropper or cotton ball.
Discard any remaining tea after 24 hours, and prepare a fresh batch as needed.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in vitamin C and flavonoids, the flowers of the dog rose are used to make teas that can help reduce inflammation and soothe styes.
Seeds: Containing high levels of essential fatty acids, the seeds can be used to make teas that help reduce redness and irritation associated with styes.
Buds: Packed with antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the buds are used to make teas that can help alleviate symptoms of styes and promote healing.
Leaves: High in tannins, the leaves of the dog rose are used to make teas that can help reduce swelling and soothe styes.
Bark: Containing astringent properties, the bark of the dog rose is used to make teas that can help dry out and reduce the size of styes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stye with herbal teas is by using chamomile or calendula tea. Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it as a compress on the affected area for 10-15 minutes, 3-4 times a day. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain. Additionally, drinking these teas can help boost the immune system, promoting faster healing. Remember to consult with a healthcare provider if symptoms persist or worsen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stye that are treated with herbal teas include blepharitis and chalazion. Blepharitis is an inflammation of the eyelids, often caused by bacteria or skin conditions, and can be alleviated with tea tree oil or chamomile tea compresses. Chalazion, a lump in the eyelid caused by a blocked oil gland, can be treated with warm compresses of calendula or goldenseal tea, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, it is important to consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
